name: TerD domain
description:
The TerD domain is found in TerD family proteins that include the paralogous TerD, TerA, TerE, TerF and TerZ proteins [[cite:PUB00008105], [cite:PUB00066658]]. It is found in a stress response operon with TerB and TerC. TerD has a maximum of two calcium binding sites [[cite:PUB00066658], [cite:PUB00054628]] depending on the conservation of aspartates [[cite:PUB00066658]]. It has various fusions to nuclease domains, RNA binding domains, ubiquitin related domains, and metal binding domains. The ter gene products lie at the centre of membrane-linked metal recognition complexes with regulatory ramifications encompassing phosphorylation-dependent signal transduction, RNA-dependent regulation, biosynthesis of nucleoside-like metabolites and DNA processing linked to novel pathways [[cite:PUB00066658]].

TerD domain is also found in cAMP binding protein, chemical-damaging agent resistance proteins and general stress proteins. The cellular Slime mould, Dictyostelium discoideum, contains a cAMP-binding protein, CABP1, which is composed of two subunits. The C-terminal half of these subunits contain this domain [[cite:PUB00008106]].

name: TM2 domain
description: This domain is composed of a pair of transmembrane α helices connected by a short linker. The function of this domain is unknown, however it occurs in a wide range or protein contexts.

name: HIRAN domain
description: The HIRAN domain (HIP116 Rad5p N-terminal) is found in the N-terminal regions of the SWI2/SNF2 proteins typified by HIP116 and Rad5p. HIRAN is found as a standalone protein in several bacteria and prophages, or fused to other catalytic domains, such as a nuclease of the restriction endonuclease fold and TDP1-like DNA phosphoesterases, in the eukaryotes [[cite:PUB00035408]]. It has been predicted that this protein functions as a DNA-binding domain that probably recognises features associated with damaged DNA or stalled replication forks [[cite:PUB00035408]].

name: Bacteriophage T5, Orf172 DNA-binding
description: This entry represents a DNA-binding domain found in bacteriophage T5, ORF172 [[cite:PUB00010111]]. The domain is related to the Bro-N and KilA-N domains that are widespread in large-DNA viruses infecting bacteria and eukaryotes  [[cite:PUB00010111]].

name: Domain of unknown function DUF2510
description: This domain of unknown function is found in a family of proteins conserved in Actinobacteria. Many members are annotated as putative membrane proteins but this could not be confirmed.

name: SNIPE associated domain
description: This entry represents the SNIPE associated domain, a small largely α-helical domain found in the SNIPE protein family. SNIPE proteins cleave phage DNA during genome injection. This domain may help SNIPE bind to phage DNA.

name: Zinc-ribbon domain
description: This entry consists of a single zinc ribbon domain. This domain is found in archaeal Large ribosomal subunit protein eL40 covering the full length of the protein. It is also found in Serine/threonine-protein kinase VRK3 found in eukaryotes, covering the N-terminal. In bacteria, this domain is found at the N-terminal in Membrane-associated protein TcaA and Uncharacterized membrane protein YvbJ.

name: Spindle pole body component Bbp1, C-terminal
description: This C-terminal domain of Bbp1, a spindle pole body component, carries coiled-coils that are necessary for the localisation of Bbp1 to the spindle pole body (SPB) [[cite:PUB00070000]]. Although not a membrane protein itself, Bbp1 binds to Mps2 as well as to Spc29 and the half-bridge protein Kar1, thus providing a model for how the SPB core is tethered within the nuclear envelope and to the half-bridge [[cite:PUB00070001]].

name: YokE-like, PH domain
description: This entry represents the PH domain found in uncharacterised proteins, including Bacillus subtilis YokE.

It has various fusions to nuclease domains, RNA binding domains, ubiquitin related domains, and metal binding domains. name: T5orf172 domain
description: This domain was identified by Iyer and colleagues [[cite:PUB00010111]].

name: Protein of unknown function (DUF2510)
description: This is family of proteins conserved in Actinobacteria. Many members are annotated as putative membrane proteins but this could not be confirmed.

name: zinc-ribbon domain
description: This family consists of a single zinc ribbon domain, ie half of a pair as in family DZR. [pfam:PF12773].

name: SNIPE associated domain
description: This entry represents the SNIPE associated domain a small largely alpha helical domain found in the SNIPE protein family. SNIPE proteins cleave phage DNA during genome injection. This domain may help SNIPE bind to phage DNA.

name: Meiotically up-regulated gene 113
description: This is a family of fungal proteins found to be up-regulated in meiosis.

name: Bacterial PH domain
description: Proteins in this family are distantly related to PH domains.
